SESSION.NAME: defaultSession
SESSION.DESCRIPTION: This diagnostic session enables the default diagnostic session in the server(s) and does not support any diagnostic application timeout handling provisions (e.g., TesterPresent service is not necessary to keep the session active). A server shall always start the default diagnostic session when powered up. If no other diagnostic session is started, then the default diagnostic session shall be running as long as the server is powered.

SESSION.NAME: programmingSession
SESSION.DESCRIPTION: This diagnosticSession enables all diagnostic services required to support the memory programming of a server. Regardless of whether the programmingSession runs in boot software or not, the ECU shall automatically suspend logging of DTCs and suspend transmission of non-diagnostic frames when entering the programmingSession.

SESSION.NAME: extendedDiagnosticSession
SESSION.DESCRIPTION: This diagnosticSession is used to enable timed diagnostic service functionality (e.g., I/O control). The diagnostic functionality in this session shall also include all supported diagnostic functionality supported in the defaultSession.

DTC_SNAPSHOT_RECORD.DESCRIPTION: The first time since DTC information was cleared or the DTC was aged that DTC status bit 3 (confirmedDTC) is set to 1.

DTC_EXTENDED_DATA_RECORD.NAME: Operation cycle counter #1
DTC_EXTENDED_DATA_RECORD.DESCRIPTION: Number of operation cycles since the DTC fault detection counter last reached its maximum value of +127 (since DTC information was last cleared). All operation cycles, including those during which the test was not completed shall be included.

DTC_EXTENDED_DATA_RECORD.NAME: Operation cycle counter #2
DTC_EXTENDED_DATA_RECORD.DESCRIPTION: Number of operation cycles since the DTC fault detection counter last reached its maximum value of +127 (since DTC information was last cleared). The operation cycles during which the test was not completed shall be excluded

DTC_EXTENDED_DATA_RECORD.NAME: Operation cycle counter #3
DTC_EXTENDED_DATA_RECORD.DESCRIPTION: Number of operation cycles since the DTC fault detection counter first reached its maximum value of +127 (since DTC information was last cleared). All operation cycles, including those in which the test has not been completed shall be included.

DTC_EXTENDED_DATA_RECORD.NAME: Operation cycle counter #4
DTC_EXTENDED_DATA_RECORD.DESCRIPTION: Number of operation cycles during which the DTC fault detection counter reached its maximum value of +127 (since DTC information was last cleared).

DTC_EXTENDED_DATA_RECORD.NAME: DTC Fault Detection Counter
DTC_EXTENDED_DATA_RECORD.DESCRIPTION: The purpose of this counter is to provide a mechanism for filtering the results of a low-level fault detection process so that test results (pass and fail) can be qualified before setting any DTC status bits.

DTC_OPERATION_CYCLES_SUPPORTED.DTC_OPERATION_CYCLE: Starts when the IPMA starts operation and all preconditions mentioned in test run / pass fail criteria are met.Ends when IPMA stops operation.

ODST: - ModuleFeatureCfg_PA_Rear = ON & (DTCEnable [235] = SELFTEST|DTCEnable[235] =BOTH) - Ign_Switch_Stable = N/A - Self Test = Test
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: - Pass: When valeo flag ERROR_LH_SENSOR_ICI = TRUE OR DTC_9B504A_FAULT_CONDITION = TRUE - Fail: When valeo flag ERROR_LH_SENSOR_ICI != TRUE OR DTC_9B504A_FAULT_CONDITION != TRUE

DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.TEST_RUN_CRITERIA: Continuous: - ModuleFeatureCfg_PA_Rear = ON & (DTCEnable[39]=CONTINUOUS|DTCEnable[39]=BOTH) - Ign_Switch_Stable = RUN - Self Test = N/A . ODST: - ModuleFeatureCfg_PA_Rear = ON & (DTCEnable [39] = SELFTEST|DTCEnable[39] =BOTH) - Ign_Switch_Stable = N/A - Self Test = Test
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FDC_PASS_FAIL_CRITERIA: - Pass: When any one of the following Valeo signal is TRUE ERROR_LH_SENSOR_DIAG_DAMP ERROR_LH_SENSOR_RESET ERROR_LH_SENSOR_RXDIAG ERRROR_LH_SENSOR_CRC ERRRO_LH_SENSOR_MEMBRANE ERRRO_LH_SENSOR_RESON_DRFT OR DTC_9B5096_FAULT_CONDITION = TRUE - Fail: When all of the following Valeo signals are FALSE ERROR_LH_SENSOR_DIAG_DAMP ERROR_LH_SENSOR_RESET ERROR_LH_SENSOR_RXDIAG ERRROR_LH_SENSOR_CRC ERRRO_LH_SENSOR_MEMBRANE ERRRO_LH_SENSOR_RESON_DRFT OR DTC_9B5096_FAULT_CONDITION != TRUE
DTC_CONTINUOUS_PARAMETERS.FAULT_SYMPTOM: Loss of AutoHitch feature

ENUM_MEMBER.DESCRIPTION: Enable HSM (i.e. HSM core is turned ON)
Disable HSM (i.e. HSM core is turned OFF)
SSWW is disabled. (i.e. Aurix host can run without needing trigger from HSM)
SSWW is Enabled. (i.e. Aurix host can run ONLY when trigger is received from HSM)
HSM UCB is configured in Development mode (example: HSM debugger interface is configured as Open. User can use JTAG is debug)
HSM UCB is configured in Production mode (example: HSM debugger interface is configured as Closed. User can not use JTAG to debug)
UCB is unlocked (i.e. UCB is not configured as locked/confirmed/OTP. UCB can be re-programmed (editable))
UCB is confirmed (i.e. UCB is configured as confirmed/OTP. Once UCB is confirmed, UCB can not be editable)

HSM UCB is configured in Production mode (example: HSM debugger interface is configured as Closed. User can not use JTAG to debug)
